# FlowTest

> FlowTest is an open-source AI-powered YAML-driven UI test runner for web, Android, and iOS. It uses Anthropic's Claude AI to execute dynamic, intent-based test steps and produces self-contained HTML reports with screenshots and video. Free to use under the MIT license.

## What is FlowTest?

FlowTest lets you write UI tests as YAML files instead of code. Declarative steps like `tapOn`, `inputText`, and `assertVisible` run instantly without AI reasoning. When the UI is dynamic or unpredictable, `ai:` steps hand control to Claude — which uses accumulated context from all prior steps (screenshots, element trees, results) to navigate toward a goal. After each run, a self-contained HTML report is generated with a health score, step timeline, screenshots per step, and video recording (web).

## Key Features

- YAML-first test authoring — no coding required for basic flows
- Claude AI drives dynamic steps using full accumulated context
- `verify:` step type for plain-language assertions the agent evaluates using AI judgment
- Cross-platform: web (agent-browser/Chrome CDP), Android (adb), iOS (idb)
- Self-contained HTML reports — no server needed, just open the file
- Video recording for web test runs
- `/refine` slash command to improve vague AI goals into detailed instructions
- Works as a Claude Code skill — install by copying a folder, no build step

## Step Types

- `tapOn` — tap an element by visible text or accessibility ID
- `inputText` — type into the focused element
- `assertVisible` / `assertNotVisible` — assert text is present or absent
- `scroll` — scroll in a direction
- `wait` — wait a number of milliseconds
- `screenshot` — take a named screenshot
- `launchApp` / `stopApp` — manage app lifecycle
- `ai` — Claude-driven block with a goal and max_steps limit
- `verify` — list of plain-language checks evaluated by the agent (UI, animation, logic)
- `when` — platform conditional (web/android/ios)

## How to Install

```bash
git clone https://github.com/govindmaheshwari2/flowtest.git
cp -r flowtest/flowtest ~/.claude/skills/flowtest
```

Then use `/flowtest` and `/refine` as slash commands in Claude Code.

## Example Flow

```yaml
flow: checkout
platform: web
app: https://demo-store.example.com

steps:
  - tapOn: "Sign in"
  - inputText: "testuser@example.com"
  - tapOn: "Log in"
  - assertVisible: "Dashboard"
  - ai:
      goal: "Add the first product to cart and complete checkout"
      max_steps: 30
  - verify:
      checks:
        - "Order confirmation message is visible"
        - "Order number is displayed on screen"
        - "Total price matches what was in the cart"
```
